zh.lifeSummary: 當Abigail Worton於1499年出生在英國瓦維其可爾其福德時，她的父親Henry Thomas Wharton24歲，母親Ann Elizabeth Warcop24歲。她於1530年與Edmund Robyns I在英國瓦維其可爾斯圖頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1550年在她的家鄉享年51歲。

nameMeanings.name: Worton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Worcestershire): habitational name from Nether and Over Worton (Oxfordshire), Worton (Wiltshire), Worton in Aysgarth (North Yorkshire), Worton Hall in Isleworth (Middlesex), or Worton in Cassington (Oxfordshire). The placenames derive from Old English wyrt ‘plant, vegetable’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’ (i.e. a kitchen garden), except for Nether and Over Worton (Oxfordshire), which derives from Old English ōra ‘edge, ridge’ + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Abigail
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘father of exaltation’ in Hebrew, borne by one of King David's wives, who had earlier been married to Nabal (1 Samuel 25:3), and by the mother of Absalom's captain Amasa (2 Samuel 1:25). The name first came into general use in Britain in the 16th century, under Puritan influence. It was a common name in literature for a lady's maid, for example in  Beaumont  and  Fletcher's  play The Scornful Lady (  1616 ). The biblical Abigail refers to herself as ‘thy servant’ in addressing King David. In Ireland this name has traditionally been used as an Anglicized form of Gobnat , although the reasons for this are not clear. It was popular in the 17th century, especially among Puritans and Nonconformists, and has again enjoyed considerable favour since the 1990s.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Abigail Worton was born in 1499, in Whichford, Warwickshire, England, her father, Henry Thomas Wharton, was 24 and her mother, Ann Elizabeth Warcop, was 24. She married Edmund Robyns I in 1530, in Stourton, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1550, in her hometown, at the age of 51.

ko.lifeSummary: Abigail Worton(이)가 1499년 영국 워윅실르 위ch퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Thomas Wharton(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Elizabeth Warcop(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1530년 영국 워윅실르 스토우르턴에서 Edmund Robyns I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1550년 그녀의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Abigail Worton родилась в 1499 г. в местности уичфорд, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry Thomas Wharton было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Ann Elizabeth Warcop, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Robyns I в 1530 г. в местности стоуртон, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1550 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 51 год.
